hero.subtitle: With the Land. With the Community. With Yourself.
hero.secondary: Close to everything, far from the ordinary
hero.bodyLine1: A sanctuary where architecture meets nature.
hero.bodyLine2: Designed for a life in connection.

about.p1: San Miguel de Allende is a UNESCO World Heritage Site, internationally recognized for its colonial architecture, vibrant cultural scene, and year-round spring-like climate.
about.p2: Home to one of the largest expat communities in Mexico with sustained real estate appreciation, San Miguel combines historical richness with an exceptional quality of life.
about.p3: A destination where Mexican tradition and contemporary sophistication converge in perfect harmony.

project.heading: The Project
project.intro: Don Diego Club Residencial is a mixed-use development integrating four unique components around Presa La Cantera — built on the land of the former Hacienda de Don Diego, just 8 minutes from the historic center.

residencial.name: Club Residencial
residencial.description: 364 residences in a 100% pedestrian environment with clubhouse, restaurants, spa, sports, and world-class amenities.

farm.name: Organic Farm & Flowers
farm.description: Organic agriculture recovering the land's original farming heritage. Orchards, seasonal flowers, and greenhouse terraces.

wellness.name: Wellness Center
wellness.description: Rehabilitation center, active retirement, and senior living with a beach club facing Presa Allende.

presa.name: Presa de la Cantera
presa.description: Lakefront boardwalk, water park, nautical club, open-air amphitheater, and waterfront commercial spaces.

location.heading: Location
location.address: Cerrada del Trébol, Carretera Celaya – Dolores Hidalgo
location.presaNote: Borders Presa Ignacio Allende and Presa La Cantera

cimenta.name: Grupo Cimienta
cimenta.description: 30+ years of experience in the full real estate development cycle.

barragan.name: Barragán Arquitectos
barragan.description: Arq. Luis Barragán Rivera — contemporary aesthetics rooted in SMA identity.

arredarq.name: Espacios Verdes / Arredarq
arredarq.description: Arq. Eliseo Arredondo — founder of the Mexican Landscape Architects Society.

artemisa.name: Artemisa Branding
artemisa.description: Visual identity and brand strategy for high-end real estate projects.

vision.body: A journey through the vision, setting, and components that shape this residential development in San Miguel de Allende.

servicesPage.heading: The Project
servicesPage.intro: Don Diego Club Residencial is built around four distinct components, united in a coherent vision of community and territory.
servicesPage.closing: Each component is designed from a clear premise: to create a residential environment that speaks to the soul of San Miguel de Allende, reinterpreted with contemporary sensitivity.

residencial.title: Club Residencial
residencial.description: 364 residences in a 100% pedestrian setting with clubhouse, restaurants, spa, sports, and world-class amenities.
residencial.supporting: A community designed for walking, gathering, and calmer living in touch with the landscape.

farm.title: Organic Farm & Flowers
farm.description: A sustainable component that restores the land’s original farming vocation with organic orchards, fruit trees, and seasonal flowers.
farm.supporting: A productive landscape that returns agricultural memory and a daily relationship with the soil.

wellness.title: Wellness Center
wellness.description: A specialized hub for rehabilitation, active retirement, and holistic wellbeing with direct access to Presa Allende.
wellness.supporting: Designed for an active-retreat experience where health, rest, and nature belong to one place.

presa.title: Presa de la Cantera
presa.description: A large public–private space that weaves nature, community, and social life along the water.
presa.supporting: The waterfront becomes a stage for recreation, gatherings, and community life.

thingsToDo.body: The club offers a wide range of activities—from gyms to restaurants and spa. Most amenities are exclusive to residents and their guests.

locationSection.body: A conscious sanctuary where luxury meets nature. Connected to the vibrant heart of the city, yet sheltered in tranquility.
locationSection.p2Desc: The heart of the city—dining and culture.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.intro: Answers to common questions about the development, investment, and services at Don Diego Club Residencial.

items.question: What is Don Diego Club Residencial and what are its components?
items.answer: It is a mixed-use residential development built around four components: Club Residencial (private residential core), Organic Farm & Flowers (productive landscape), Wellness Center (holistic wellbeing and senior living), and Presa de la Cantera (public–private waterfront destination).

items.question: Where is the development located?
items.answer: It is strategically located about 8 minutes from the historic center of San Miguel de Allende, on the Celaya – Dolores Hidalgo highway. It borders Presa Ignacio Allende and Presa La Cantera.

items.question: Is access to all components exclusive to residents?
items.answer: Club Residencial and Organic Farm are exclusive and prioritized for owners and residents. Presa de la Cantera is conceived as a destination open to the San Miguel community, blending nature and social life with cultural and recreational activities.

items.question: How does the productive side of “Organic Farm & Flowers” work?
items.answer: It restores the land’s farming vocation through organic agriculture and seasonal flower production. Beyond aesthetics, fresh products feed restaurants and daily life on the development, with organic gardens, greenhouses, and production fields linked by paths and bike lanes.

items.question: What is the pedestrian-focused urban design?
items.answer: The interior of the development is 100% pedestrian, keeping cars out of the inner streets. A peripheral road circuit at grade change handles vehicles, while parking is covered with direct pedestrian access to homes—two spaces per unit.

slides.title: CLUB RESIDENCIAL — ARCHITECTURAL DESIGN
slides.description: A space designed for community life, combining interior comfort with privileged views and sophisticated contemporary furnishings.

slides.title: HOME — MATERIALS & TEXTURES
slides.description: Designed for comfort and gathering, balancing ease, elegance, and function.

slides.title: NATURAL LIGHT — FINISHES
slides.description: Warm tones and subtle details that add tactile rhythm and timeless charm to modern interiors.

slides.title: PRESA DE LA CANTERA — NATURAL SETTING
slides.description: Seamless integration with the local landscape, with unmatched reservoir views and deep respect for topography.

slides.title: THE LANDSCAPE — NATIVE PLANTING
slides.description: Expansive areas designed to connect organically with surrounding nature, maximizing serenity outdoors.

slides.title: FLUID SPACES — SPATIAL HARMONY
slides.description: Natural transitions between living areas guided by an earth-tone palette and exceptional craftsmanship.

slides.title: MAIN ENTRANCE — WELCOME & PRESENCE
slides.description: A majestic entrance that frames the residential experience with structure, character, and rural elegance.

slides.title: ORGANIC GARDEN — SUSTAINABILITY
slides.description: Cultivable spaces for fresh harvests, promoting a sustainable lifestyle rooted in the land.

slides.title: OUTDOOR TERRACE — OPEN-AIR LIVING
slides.description: Outdoor lounges that extend indoor comfort outside, inviting gatherings under open skies.

slides.title: AERIAL VIEW — MASTER PLAN
slides.description: A privileged perspective of the whole project, showing thoughtful architecture within the local ecosystem.
